classifications.phrase: inflation is still some way above the midpoint of the 2–3 per cent target range
our current forecasts do not see inflation returning sustainably to target until 2026
underlying inflation has been above the midpoint of the target for 11 consecutive quarters
policy is currently restrictive and working broadly as anticipated
underlying inflation is more indicative of inflation momentum, and it remains too high
Policy will need to be sufficiently restrictive until the Board is confident that inflation is…

payload.rationale: The RBA signals a hawkish stance by emphasizing persistent above-target inflation, weak growth, and the need for continued restrictive policy, while acknowledging some labour market softening.
